passage.title: Hallway Sign Project
passage.text: The school helper team wanted younger students to find morning stations more easily. At first, the team made one colorful map with many tiny labels, but the labels were too small to read quickly. Several first graders said "The map looks nice, but I cannot find the next table fast." After hearing that, the team grouped each station with one simple symbol and placed matching floor dots under the signs. They also added short reminder cards at each corner so students could check their direction again when they forgot the first step. On the next test day, most students reached the reading, math, and writing stations without extra help. The team noticed that clear direction clues worked better than pretty decoration. Because of that result, the school decided to update the rest of the hallway in the same clearer style.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
Improving school signs so younger students can follow stations
Painting a room with brighter colors
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The design looked nice but felt confusing
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team add at each corner?
choices: Short reminder cards
Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does crowded mean in the second sentence?
choices: A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
Too full and hard to read
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
The new signs gave students clearer directions at each step
It made the room much larger
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Ask students to memorize everything
Use the same clearer style in more school areas
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
